papers.title: TurboServe: Serving Streaming Video Generation Efficiently and Economically
papers.abstract: Abstract TurboServe is a specialized serving system for streaming video generation that addresses session state management and dynamic resource allocation challenges through integrated scheduling, autoscaling, and migration mechanisms.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Streaming video generation is emerging as a new serving workload in which users interact with long-lived sessions that generate video progressively, chunk by chunk. Unlike offline video generation or typical LLM serving, streaming video generation must preserve session state across active and idle periods, repeatedly schedule ongoing sessions, and deliver each chunk under a tight latency target. This creates two key serving challenges in multi-user, multi-GPU environments: session duration heterogeneity, where long-running sessions make placement decisions suboptimal over time, and temporal user-demand heterogeneity, where the number of active sessions fluctuates sharply across bursts and idle periods.   We present TurboServe, the first serving system designed specifically for streaming video generation workloads. TurboServe formulates serving as an online scheduling problem that jointly coordinates session placement and GPU provisioning. Its closed-loop scheduling algorithm combines a migration-aware placement controller, which rebalances sessions across GPUs to reduce the maximum per-chunk latency, with a load-driven autoscaling controller, which adapts the GPU budget to workload variation for improved cost efficiency. To support these decisions at runtime, TurboServe implements coalesced chunk processing for batching concurrent active sessions on the same GPU, GPU-CPU offloading for session suspension and resumption, and NCCL-based GPU-GPU migration for online rebalancing. We evaluate TurboServe on real-world production traces from Shengshu Technology across multiple model sizes and GPU clusters with up to 64 NVIDIA B300 GPUs. Compared with baseline serving configurations, TurboServe reduces worst-case per-chunk latency by 37.5% and total GPU operating cost by 37.2% on average. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/shengshu-ai/TurboServe.

ru.title: Эффективная потоковая генерация видео через умное планирование и балансировку GPU
ru.desc: TurboServe — это специализированная система для обслуживания потоковой генерации видео, которая решает задачу управления состоянием сессий и динамического распределения ресурсов. Система формулирует проблему обслуживания как задачу онлайн-планирования, координирующую размещение сессий и выделение GPU. Алгоритм замкнутого цикла TurboServe объединяет контроллер размещения с учётом миграции, перебалансирующий сессии между GPU, и контроллер автомасштабирования, адаптирующий бюджет GPU к вариациям нагрузки. Система достигает снижения максимальной задержки обработки на 37.5% и снижения операционных затрат GPU на 37.2% в среднем.

en.title: TurboServe: Optimizing Streaming Video Generation with Smart Resource Management
en.desc: TurboServe is a novel serving system designed specifically for streaming video generation, which involves managing long-lived user sessions that produce video in real-time. It tackles challenges like session state management and dynamic resource allocation by employing integrated scheduling, autoscaling, and migration techniques. The system uses a closed-loop scheduling algorithm that optimizes session placement across GPUs and adjusts GPU resources based on workload demands. Evaluation results show that TurboServe significantly reduces latency and operational costs compared to traditional serving methods, making it efficient for handling fluctuating user demands.

zh.title: TurboServe：流媒体视频生成的高效服务解决方案
zh.desc: TurboServe 是一个专门为流媒体视频生成设计的服务系统，解决了会话状态管理和动态资源分配的挑战。它通过集成调度、自动扩展和迁移机制来优化多用户和多 GPU 环境中的服务效率。TurboServe 将服务问题视为在线调度问题，协调会话放置和 GPU 资源配置，以降低每个视频片段的延迟。通过实验证明，TurboServe 在实际生产环境中显著降低了延迟和 GPU 运营成本。

papers.title: Domain Arithmetic: One-Shot VLA Adaptation under Environmental Shifts
papers.abstract: Abstract Vision-Language-Action models can be efficiently adapted to new environments using a single demonstration through weight vector arithmetic that isolates domain-specific information via subspace alignment.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models often fail to perform the same learned tasks under environmental shifts, such as changes in camera pose and shifts to a different but similar robot (e.g., from Panda to UR5e). Adapting these models to the shifted environment (i.e., target domain) often requires training on multiple demonstrations for each task, which are costly to collect. To reduce the burden of data curation and training, we propose an analogy-based method that adapts VLA models under environmental shifts through weight vector arithmetic with domain-specific information addition, named Domain ARiThmetic (DART). Unlike prior approaches, DART requires collecting only a single demonstration, enabling efficient adaptation. To accurately isolate domain-specific information for addition, DART performs subspace alignment between singular components in weight vectors to filter out noisy components. In both simulated and real-world experiments, DART outperforms existing VLA adaptation methods in one-shot scenarios across diverse visual and embodiment shifts. Code is available at https://github.com/snumprlab/dart.

ru.title: Адаптация мультимодальных моделей одной демонстрацией через арифметику весов
ru.desc: Работа предлагает метод DART для адаптации Vision-Language-Action моделей к новым окружениям, использующий арифметику весовых векторов и выделение доменоспецифичной информации через выравнивание подпространств. VLA модели обычно теряют способность выполнять задачи при изменении окружения, таком как сдвиг камеры или переход на другого робота, что требует сбора множества демонстраций для переобучения. DART решает эту проблему, требуя только одну демонстрацию благодаря изолированию релевантных компонентов весов через анализ сингулярных разложений. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что метод превосходит существующие подходы в сценариях one-shot адаптации при различных визуальных и морфологических сдвигах.

en.title: Efficient One-Shot Adaptation for VLA Models with DART
en.desc: This paper introduces Domain ARiThmetic (DART), a novel method for adapting Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models to new environments using only a single demonstration. DART utilizes weight vector arithmetic to isolate and add domain-specific information, allowing for effective adaptation even when faced with environmental shifts like changes in camera angles or different robot types. By performing subspace alignment on weight vectors, DART filters out irrelevant noise, enhancing the model's performance in one-shot scenarios. Experimental results show that DART significantly outperforms traditional adaptation methods, making it a more efficient solution for real-world applications.

zh.title: 单次示例，高效适应环境变化！
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为DART的方法，用于在环境变化下高效适应视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型。通过权重向量算术，DART能够仅使用单个示例来调整模型，减少了数据收集和训练的负担。该方法通过子空间对齐来隔离领域特定信息，从而过滤掉噪声成分。实验结果表明，DART在多种视觉和实体变化的单次适应场景中优于现有的VLA适应方法。

papers.title: CausalMix: Data Mixture as Causal Inference for Language Model Training
papers.abstract: Abstract CausalMix addresses limitations in LLM data mixing by formulating mixture optimization as a causal inference problem, enabling dynamic adaptation to shifting data distributions without costly retraining.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct In Large Language Model (LLM) training, data mixing plays a pivotal role in determining model performance. Recent methods optimize mixture weights via proxy models, but they rely on the assumption of static data distributions. As a result, when the underlying data pool shifts, these methods require costly retraining from scratch. This limitation restricts their ability to scale seamlessly from small settings to larger data pools and model sizes. In this paper, we propose CausalMix to address this limitation by casting data mixture optimization as a causal inference problem. We formulate the statistical features of the data pool as covariates and the domain mixture as the treatment. After fitting a causal model on 512 runs of Qwen2.5-0.5B to estimate the Conditional Average Treatment Effect (CATE), we extrapolate the optimal mixture for an 800K data pool and apply it to train a 7B model. Furthermore, we successfully generalize the framework to long chain-of-thought data on Qwen3-4B-Base. By leveraging causal modeling to isolate confounding biases, CausalMix dynamically infers state-dependent optimal data mixtures. Extensive experiments show that the mixture guided by CausalMix consistently improves performance across multiple downstream tasks, outperforming RegMix and other baselines. In addition, we use the CATE Interpreter to provide visual analysis of the learned mixing strategy. Overall, CausalMix offers a causal and interpretable framework for optimizing LLM data mixtures.

ru.title: Причинный вывод для адаптивного смешивания данных в обучении LLM
ru.desc: Статья предлагает CausalMix — метод оптимизации смешивания данных при обучении больших языковых моделей, сформулированный как задача причинного вывода. Вместо традиционного подхода с использованием прокси-моделей, который предполагает неизменность распределения данных, авторы используют причинное моделирование для оценки условного среднего эффекта воздействия (CATE). Это позволяет динамически адаптировать оптимальные пропорции смешивания данных без дорогостоящего переобучения модели с нуля при изменении состава данных. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что CausalMix стабильно улучшает производительность на различных задачах и обеспечивает интерпретируемость стратегии смешивания.

en.title: Dynamic Data Mixing through Causal Inference
en.desc: CausalMix introduces a new approach to optimize data mixing in large language models (LLMs) by treating it as a causal inference problem. This method allows for dynamic adjustments to data mixtures in response to changing data distributions, eliminating the need for expensive retraining. By using causal modeling to estimate the Conditional Average Treatment Effect (CATE), CausalMix can effectively identify the best data mixtures for different scenarios. The results demonstrate that this framework significantly enhances model performance across various tasks compared to traditional methods.

zh.title: CausalMix：动态优化数据混合的因果推断方法
zh.desc: CausalMix 通过将数据混合优化视为因果推断问题，解决了大型语言模型（LLM）数据混合的局限性。该方法能够动态适应数据分布的变化，而无需昂贵的重新训练。我们将数据池的统计特征视为协变量，将领域混合视为处理，进而估计条件平均处理效应（CATE）。实验结果表明，CausalMix 指导的混合策略在多个下游任务中持续提升了模型性能，超越了其他基线方法。

papers.title: Perceive-to-Reason: Decoupling Perception and Reasoning for Fine-Grained Visual Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract A unified framework named Perceive-to-Reason (P2R) is introduced that separates visual perception from reasoning in vision-language models through a two-stage process, improving fine-grained visual reasoning performance on high-resolution images.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Fine-grained visual reasoning remains challenging for vision-language models, especially when small but critical visual cues are buried in high-resolution images. Existing approaches rely on repeated cropping or test-time visual search to introduce local evidence, but they typically do not explicitly distinguish perception from reasoning. In this paper, we propose Perceive-to-Reason (P2R), a unified framework that formulates fine-grained visual reasoning as a two-stage process: the model first localizes question-relevant evidence as a Perceiver, and then answers the question as a Reasoner based on the annotated image and cropped regions. To better align training with this decoupled formulation, we further introduce Perception-Reasoning Alternating GRPO (PRA-GRPO), a role-aware reinforcement learning strategy that alternates between perception-focused and reasoning-focused updates using only final-answer supervision. Built on top of Qwen3-VL-Instruct-2B/4B/8B, P2R consistently improves performance across model scales. In particular, P2R-4B achieves 93.2% on V-Star, 81.9% on HR-Bench-4K, and 80.5% on HR-Bench-8K, substantially outperforming its corresponding backbone. Further experiments show that the benefits of P2R extend beyond high-resolution benchmarks to broader multimodal reasoning tasks. These results suggest that explicitly decoupling perception from reasoning provides an effective framework for fine-grained visual reasoning.

ru.title: Разделяй восприятие и рассуждение для точного понимания изображений
ru.desc: В статье предложена объединённая архитектура Perceive-to-Reason (P2R), которая разделяет задачи визуального восприятия и логического рассуждения в vision-language моделях на два отдельных этапа. На первом этапе модель выступает в роли Perceiver, локализуя релевантные для ответа визуальные детали на высокого разрешения изображениях, а на втором этапе работает как Reasoner, давая ответ на основе аннотированного изображения и вырезанных областей. Авторы разработали специальную стратегию обучения PRA-GRPO, использующую усиленное обучение с ролевой фокусировкой, которая чередует обновления, сосредоточенные на восприятии и рассуждении. Экспериментальные результаты показывают значительное улучшение производительности на различных бенчмарках для высокого разрешения и демонстрируют эффективность явного разделения восприятия от рассуждения для детального визуального анализа.

en.title: Decoupling Perception and Reasoning for Better Visual Understanding
en.desc: The paper introduces a new framework called Perceive-to-Reason (P2R) that enhances visual reasoning in vision-language models by separating the processes of visual perception and reasoning. It operates in two stages: first, the model identifies relevant visual evidence, and then it uses this information to answer questions. This approach addresses challenges in fine-grained visual reasoning, especially in high-resolution images where important details may be overlooked. Additionally, the authors propose a reinforcement learning strategy, PRA-GRPO, to optimize the training process by alternating between perception and reasoning updates, leading to improved performance across various benchmarks.

zh.title: 视觉推理的新框架：感知与推理分离
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为Perceive-to-Reason (P2R) 的统一框架，旨在将视觉感知与推理分开，以提高视觉语言模型在高分辨率图像上的细粒度视觉推理性能。该框架通过两阶段过程进行：首先，模型作为感知者定位与问题相关的证据，然后作为推理者基于标注图像和裁剪区域回答问题。为了更好地与这种解耦的训练方式对齐，本文还引入了一种角色感知的强化学习策略PRA-GRPO，交替进行以感知为中心和以推理为中心的更新。实验结果表明，P2R在多个模型规模上均表现出色，尤其在高分辨率基准测试和更广泛的多模态推理任务中，显示出显著的性能提升。

papers.title: MemSyco-Bench: Benchmarking Sycophancy in Agent Memory
papers.abstract: Abstract Memory plays a crucial role in LLM-based agents, but retrieved memories can cause sycophancy issues where agents over-align with users at the expense of factual accuracy, necessitating new evaluation benchmarks that assess memory's impact on reasoning and decision-making rather than just storage and retrieval.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Memory has emerged as a cornerstone of modern LLM-based agents, supporting their evolution from single-turn assistants to long-term collaborators. However, memory is not always beneficial: retrieved memories often induce a critical issue of sycophancy, causing agents to over-align with the user at the cost of factual accuracy or objective reasoning. Despite this emerging risk, existing memory benchmarks primarily evaluate whether memories are correctly stored, retrieved, or updated, while overlooking how retrieved memories influence downstream reasoning and decision-making. To bridge this gap, we propose MemSyco-Bench, a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating memory-induced sycophancy in agent systems. MemSyco-Bench measures when memory should influence a decision and how valid memory should be used. Specifically, it covers five tasks that assess whether agents can reject memory as factual evidence, respect its applicable scope, resolve conflicts between memory and objective evidence, track memory updates, and use valid memory for personalization. All related resources are collected for the community at https://github.com/XMUDeepLIT/MemSyco-Bench.

ru.title: Критическая оценка влияния памяти на объективность рассуждений агентов
ru.desc: В статье исследуется проблема сыкофантии в агентах на основе LLM, которая возникает когда система чрезмерно согласуется с пользователем в ущерб фактической точности. Авторы указывают, что существующие бенчмарки для оценки памяти фокусируются только на сохранении и извлечении информации, игнорируя влияние полученной памяти на дальнейшее рассуждение и принятие решений. Для решения этой проблемы предложен новый бенчмарк MemSyco-Bench, который комплексно оценивает влияние памяти на проявление сыкофантии в системах агентов. Бенчмарк включает пять задач, проверяющих способность агентов отвергать память как доказательство, соблюдать границы её применимости, разрешать конфликты между памятью и объективными фактами, отслеживать обновления памяти и использовать валидную память для персонализации.

en.title: Evaluating Memory's Impact on LLM Decision-Making
en.desc: This paper discusses the importance of memory in large language model (LLM) agents, highlighting how it can lead to sycophancy, where agents overly conform to user preferences at the expense of accuracy. The authors argue that current benchmarks for evaluating memory in LLMs focus too much on storage and retrieval, neglecting how memory affects reasoning and decision-making. To address this issue, they introduce MemSyco-Bench, a new benchmark designed to evaluate the impact of memory on sycophancy in agent systems. This benchmark includes tasks that assess an agent's ability to discern when to rely on memory, manage conflicts with factual evidence, and utilize memory for personalized interactions.

zh.title: 评估智能体记忆的阿谀奉承现象
zh.desc: 在基于大语言模型的智能体中，记忆扮演着重要角色，但检索到的记忆可能导致阿谀奉承的问题，使得智能体过度迎合用户，从而牺牲事实准确性。因此，需要新的评估基准来评估记忆对推理和决策的影响，而不仅仅是存储和检索。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了MemSyco-Bench，这是一个全面的基准，用于评估智能体系统中的记忆引发的阿谀奉承现象。该基准涵盖五个任务，评估智能体如何处理记忆与客观证据之间的冲突，以及如何有效利用有效的记忆进行个性化。

papers.title: BioInsight: Multi-Agent Orchestration for Interactive Biomedical Knowledge Discovery
papers.abstract: Abstract BioInsight is a multi-agent system that transforms static biomedical reports into interactive, evidence-centered interfaces by organizing disease-specific evidence through structured artifacts and deterministic citation normalization.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Biomedical researchers increasingly use AI-generated analyses and reports to interpret protein-level signals, but static outputs are often insufficient for research decision-making, where users need to inspect evidence, assess uncertainty, compare mechanisms, and refine hypotheses. We present BioInsight, a multi-agent system that moves from static biomedical report generation to interactive evidence-centered interactive interface generation. Given a disease name, a protein association table, and optional cohort metadata, BioInsight organizes disease-specific evidence through typed intermediate artifacts, including ranked pathways, literature evidence packets, protein-level reasoning notes, citation-grounded reports, dashboard schemas, and rendered interactive interfaces. The system decomposes evidence retrieval from mechanistic reasoning, normalizes citations through deterministic components, and converts the same structured evidence used in the report into an interactive interface. We evaluate BioInsight on standardized biomedical QA, challenging protein-function reasoning, and end-to-end biomedical evidence synthesis. Results show that BioInsight achieves best, and suggest that biomedical AI systems should move beyond text-only and static reports toward provenance-preserving, interactive evidence artifacts.

ru.title: От статических отчетов к интерактивным доказательствам: мультиагентная система для биомедицинских исследований
ru.desc: BioInsight — это мультиагентная система, которая преобразует статические биомедицинские отчеты в интерактивные интерфейсы, центрированные на доказательствах. Система организует болезнь-специфичные доказательства через типизированные артефакты, включая ранжированные пути, литературные пакеты доказательств и нормализованные цитирования. Ключевая особенность — разделение задач извлечения доказательств и механистического рассуждения, что позволяет использовать одни и те же структурированные данные как для отчетов, так и для интерактивных интерфейсов. Система продемонстрировала высокую производительность на задачах биомедицинского анализа и синтеза доказательств, предлагая исследователям инструмент для проверки доказательств и уточнения гипотез.

en.title: Transforming Static Reports into Interactive Evidence Interfaces
en.desc: BioInsight is a multi-agent system designed to enhance the usability of biomedical reports by transforming them into interactive interfaces that focus on evidence. It organizes disease-specific information using structured artifacts and ensures accurate citation normalization. This system allows researchers to engage with the data more effectively, enabling them to assess uncertainty, compare mechanisms, and refine their hypotheses. Evaluation results indicate that BioInsight outperforms traditional static reports, highlighting the need for interactive and provenance-preserving tools in biomedical AI.

zh.title: 生物医学报告的互动转型
zh.desc: BioInsight 是一个多智能体系统，旨在将静态生物医学报告转化为互动的、以证据为中心的界面。该系统通过结构化的中间产物和确定性的引用规范化，组织特定疾病的证据。BioInsight 允许用户在研究决策中检查证据、评估不确定性、比较机制和完善假设。评估结果表明，BioInsight 在生物医学问答、蛋白质功能推理和生物医学证据综合方面表现最佳，强调了生物医学 AI 系统应向互动证据产物发展。

papers.title: ASPIRE: Agentic /Skills Discovery for Robotics
papers.abstract: Abstract ASPIRE is a continual learning system that autonomously develops and refines robot control programs through iterative exploration, achieving superior performance and zero-shot generalization in manipulation and household tasks while enabling sim-to-real transfer.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Traditional robot programming is challenging: it requires orchestrating multimodal perception, managing physical contact dynamics, and handling diverse configurations and execution failures. We introduce ASPIRE (Agentic Skill Programming through Iterative Robot Exploration), a continual learning system that autonomously writes and refines robot control programs in a code-as-policy paradigm while compounding experience into a reusable skill library. ASPIRE discovers skills that persist across tasks, simulation and real-world settings, and embodiments. It operates in an open-ended loop with three components: (1) a closed-loop robot execution engine that exposes fine-grained multimodal traces, enabling autonomous failure diagnosis, repair synthesis, and validation; (2) a continually expanding skill library that distills validated fixes into reusable, transferable knowledge; and (3) evolutionary search that generates diverse task sequences and control programs to explore beyond single-trajectory refinement. ASPIRE surpasses prior methods by up to 77% on LIBERO-Pro manipulation under perturbation, 72% on Robosuite bimanual handover, and 32% on BEHAVIOR-1K long-horizon household tasks. Its accumulated library also enables zero-shot generalization to unseen long-horizon tasks: on LIBERO-Pro Long, ASPIRE achieves 31% success versus 4% for prior methods despite their use of test-time reasoning and retries. Finally, simulation-discovered skills provide initial evidence of sim-to-real transfer, substantially reducing real-robot programming effort across different embodiments and robot APIs.

ru.title: Робот учится программировать себя: непрерывное обучение через исследование и переиспользование навыков
ru.desc: ASPIRE — это система непрерывного обучения, которая автоматически разрабатывает и совершенствует управляющие программы для робота через итеративное исследование окружающей среды. Система использует парадигму code-as-policy, где программы контроля записываются на коде и накапливаются в переиспользуемую библиотеку навыков. ASPIRE содержит три компонента: механизм замкнутого цикла выполнения с диагностикой отказов, расширяющуюся библиотеку проверенных навыков и эволюционный поиск для генерации разнообразных программ и последовательностей задач. Система достигает превосходной производительности на бенчмарках манипуляции и бытовых задач, обеспечивает нулевую обобщение на невиданные задачи и успешный перенос навыков из симуляции в реальный мир.

en.title: Empowering Robots with Autonomous Skill Development
en.desc: ASPIRE is a continual learning system designed for robots that autonomously creates and improves control programs through exploration. It utilizes a code-as-policy approach, allowing it to build a library of reusable skills that can be applied across various tasks and environments. The system features a closed-loop execution engine for diagnosing and fixing failures, an expanding skill library for knowledge transfer, and an evolutionary search mechanism for exploring diverse task sequences. ASPIRE demonstrates significant performance improvements in manipulation tasks and enables zero-shot generalization to new tasks, showcasing its effectiveness in both simulation and real-world applications.

zh.title: ASPIRE：自主学习与技能转移的机器人控制系统
zh.desc: ASPIRE是一个持续学习系统，能够通过迭代探索自主开发和优化机器人控制程序。它在操作和家庭任务中实现了卓越的性能和零样本泛化，同时支持从模拟到现实的转移。ASPIRE采用代码作为策略的范式，积累经验形成可重用的技能库，发现跨任务的持久技能。该系统通过闭环执行引擎、不断扩展的技能库和进化搜索三大组件，超越了以往方法，显著提高了机器人操作的效率和灵活性。

papers.title: The State-Prediction Separation Hypothesis
papers.abstract: Abstract Separating state prediction from token prediction in Transformers improves language modeling performance and efficiency across different scales.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Transformers use the same forward computation stream to both predict the next token and store useful state for future token predictions. We formulate the state-prediction separation hypothesis: disentangling the two roles yields better language modeling performance. We design a Transformer variant that uses two computation streams to separate the two functions, and conduct pretraining experiments across various scales. Our experiments show that state-prediction separation consistently offers better data and compute efficiencies, improving validation loss and outperforming standard Transformers by 2--3 percentage points on average on downstream tasks. We also conduct extensive empirical analysis that rules out potential confounders and demonstrates the fundamental difference in the gradients our design entails.

ru.title: Разделение предсказания состояния и токена для эффективного языкового моделирования
ru.desc: В статье предлагается новый подход к архитектуре Трансформеров, который разделяет предсказание следующего токена от сохранения состояния для будущих предсказаний. Авторы выдвигают гипотезу о том, что использование двух отдельных потоков вычислений для этих функций улучшает качество языкового моделирования. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что такое разделение обеспечивает лучшую эффективность по данным и вычислительным ресурсам, а также превосходит стандартные Трансформеры на 2-3 процентных пункта на задачах fine-tuning. Проведённый анализ градиентов подтверждает фундаментальные различия в механизме обучения предложенной архитектуры.

en.title: Enhancing Transformers: Separate State and Token Predictions for Better Performance
en.desc: This paper introduces a new approach to improve language modeling in Transformers by separating state prediction from token prediction. By using two distinct computation streams, the model can better manage the roles of predicting the next token and storing useful information for future predictions. The authors conducted experiments that showed this separation leads to enhanced performance and efficiency, achieving lower validation loss and outperforming traditional Transformers on various tasks. Their findings suggest that this method not only improves results but also provides better data and compute efficiency across different model sizes.

zh.title: 分离状态预测与标记预测，提升语言建模效率
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种新的Transformer变体，通过将状态预测与标记预测分离来提高语言建模的性能和效率。我们假设这种分离可以改善模型的表现，并设计了一个使用两个计算流的Transformer，以实现这两个功能的解耦。实验结果表明，状态预测的分离在不同规模的预训练中都能显著提高数据和计算效率，验证损失降低，并在下游任务中平均超越标准Transformer 2-3个百分点。我们还进行了广泛的实证分析，排除了潜在的混淆因素，展示了我们设计所带来的梯度的根本差异。

papers.title: ABot-M0.5: Unified Mobility-and-Manipulation World Action Model
papers.abstract: Abstract ABot-M0.5 is a World Action Model for mobile manipulation that improves performance through temporal granularity alignment, action space disentanglement, and train-test consistency in autoregressive prediction.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Mobile manipulation is a key capability for general-purpose robots, yet remains challenging for current embodied learning methods. VLA policies are typically reactive and lack explicit world modeling, while existing World Action Models (WAMs) are still poorly aligned with the structure of mobile manipulation: they operate on coarse video chunks, model entangled navigation-manipulation actions, and train inverse dynamics under supervision that does not match autoregressive inference. As a result, they often miss fine-grained contact dynamics, suffer from action-distribution conflicts, and accumulate errors over long-horizon rollouts. We propose ABot-M0.5, a new WAM built on the insight that mobile manipulation requires alignment at three levels: temporal granularity, action space, and train-test consistency. To align temporal granularity, we introduce intermediate latent actions that capture local visual state transitions and serve as an bridging action space between video latents and embodiment-specific controls. To align action space, we design a dual-level Mixture-of-Transformers architecture that disentangles both modality representations and heterogeneous action subspaces such as base movement and arm manipulation. To align inference conditions, we propose the dream-forcing training strategy that progressively trains inverse dynamics on model-predicted videos, improving train-test alignment and robustness during autoregressive prediction. Experiments on challenging mobile and fine-grained manipulation benchmarks demonstrate that ABot-M0.5 achieves state-of-the-art performance in both long-horizon task success and finegrained control accuracy. These results highlight the critical importance of granularity-aligned, action-disentangled, and inference-consistent world-action modeling.

ru.title: Согласованное моделирование мира для мобильной манипуляции роботов
ru.desc: ABot-M0.5 представляет собой модель мира для мобильной манипуляции роботов, которая решает проблемы существующих подходов путём согласования на трёх уровнях: временной гранулярности, пространства действий и консистентности между обучением и инференсом. Модель использует промежуточные скрытые действия для захвата локальных переходов визуального состояния и архитектуру Mixture-of-Transformers для разделения представлений модальностей и разнородных подпространств действий. Для улучшения предсказаний авторегрессионной модели предложена стратегия dream-forcing, которая обучает инверсную динамику на предсказанных видеокадрах. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что ABot-M0.5 достигает современной производительности на задачах мобильной манипуляции с длинным горизонтом и точным управлением.

en.title: Aligning Actions for Smarter Mobile Manipulation
en.desc: ABot-M0.5 is a World Action Model designed to enhance mobile manipulation in robots by addressing key challenges in embodied learning. It introduces a novel approach that aligns temporal granularity, disentangles action spaces, and ensures consistency between training and testing phases. By using intermediate latent actions and a dual-level Mixture-of-Transformers architecture, it effectively captures the complexities of navigation and manipulation tasks. The proposed dream-forcing training strategy further improves the model's robustness and accuracy, leading to state-of-the-art performance in long-horizon tasks and fine-grained control.

zh.title: 移动操作的智能新突破
zh.desc: ABot-M0.5是一种针对移动操作的世界动作模型，通过时间粒度对齐、动作空间解耦和训练-测试一致性来提高性能。该模型引入了中间潜在动作，以捕捉局部视觉状态的变化，并作为视频潜在空间与具体控制之间的桥梁。它采用双层混合变换器架构，解耦不同的模态表示和异构动作子空间，从而优化动作空间。实验结果表明，ABot-M0.5在长时间任务成功率和精细控制准确性方面达到了最先进的性能，强调了粒度对齐、动作解耦和推理一致性在世界动作建模中的重要性。

papers.title: AutoTrainess: Teaching Language Models to Improve Language Models Autonomously
papers.abstract: Abstract AutoTrainess enables autonomous language model training by providing structured agent-computer interfaces that guide planning, data preparation, training, evaluation, and logging operations more effectively than traditional command-line approaches.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Training language models (LMs) remains a highly human-intensive process, even as frontier language model agents become increasingly capable at software engineering and other long-horizon tasks. A central challenge is that autonomous post-training is not just a coding problem: it requires the agent to repeatedly plan iterations, construct benchmark-aligned data, run stable training jobs, evaluate checkpoints, and preserve experiment state across many hours of interaction. We present AutoTrainess, a LM agent that exposes these operations as a repository of agent-computer interfaces for planning, data preparation, training, evaluation, and logging. Rather than leaving the agent to operate in a raw CLI environment with an underspecified action space, AutoTrainess externalizes prior human experience as explicit workflows, rules, and execution constraints that guide the agent toward effective and reliable training behavior. On PostTrainBench, AutoTrainess consistently outperforms CLI-only baselines, achieving 26.94 average score with GPT-5.4 (Codex) versus 23.21 for CLI-only. It also generalizes across models and harnesses, improving DeepSeek-V4-Flash (OpenCode) from 12.13 to 19.58.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Структурированные интерфейсы для автономного обучения языковых моделей
ru.desc: AutoTrainess — это система, которая позволяет языковым моделям самостоятельно обучаться, предоставляя им структурированные интерфейсы взаимодействия для планирования, подготовки данных, обучения, оценки и логирования. Вместо того чтобы работать в сыром окружении командной строки, агент использует явно определённые рабочие процессы и правила, закодированные на основе опыта человека. Система значительно улучшает качество обучения: на бенчмарке PostTrainBench AutoTrainess показала среднюю оценку 26.94 с GPT-5.4, в то время как подход с использованием только CLI достиг лишь 23.21. Методология успешно обобщается на разные модели и архитектуры, значительно повышая производительность при обучении.

en.title: Empowering Language Model Training with AutoTrainess
en.desc: AutoTrainess is a framework designed to enhance the training of language models by automating various tasks that typically require human intervention. It provides structured interfaces that help agents manage planning, data preparation, training, evaluation, and logging more efficiently than traditional command-line methods. By externalizing human expertise into workflows and rules, AutoTrainess guides agents to perform training operations reliably and effectively. The results show that AutoTrainess significantly outperforms command-line only approaches, demonstrating its ability to improve training outcomes across different models.

zh.title: 自主训练语言模型的新方法
zh.desc: AutoTrainess 是一种自主语言模型训练的方法，它通过提供结构化的代理-计算机接口来更有效地指导规划、数据准备、训练、评估和日志记录操作。尽管前沿语言模型代理在软件工程等任务中变得越来越强大，但训练语言模型仍然是一个高度依赖人力的过程。AutoTrainess 通过将这些操作外部化为明确的工作流程、规则和执行约束，帮助代理在训练过程中表现出更有效和可靠的行为。实验结果表明，AutoTrainess 在 PostTrainBench 上的表现优于传统的命令行方法，显示出其在模型训练中的优势。

papers.title: Valdi: Value Diffusion World Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Value Diffusion World Models combine end-to-end online training with latent diffusion dynamics to enable fast, uncertain dynamics prediction for Model Predictive Control in reinforcement learning environments.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct World models can enable Model Predictive Control (MPC), but this requires dynamics prediction that is both fast enough for online use and expressive enough to represent uncertain futures. Diffusion models offer a natural mechanism for modeling uncertain dynamics, yet their iterative inference procedure makes them difficult to use for low-latency latent planning. We bridge this gap with Value Diffusion World Models (Valdi), combining end-to-end online training for MPC with a latent diffusion dynamics model. In preliminary experiments on the CarRacing environment, we show that Valdi, using a single diffusion step at both training and inference, matches a deterministic MLP baseline. Our experiments expose a trade-off between predictive multimodality and control performance in this setup. Code is available at https://github.com/Kit115/ValueDiffusionWorldModels.
affiliations: KE:SAI Kyutai ELLIS Scalable Autonomous Intelligence, Germany

ru.title: Быстрая диффузия для предсказательного управления в реальном времени
ru.desc: В работе предложен подход Value Diffusion World Models, который объединяет диффузионные модели для предсказания динамики окружения с онлайн-обучением в задачах управления с предсказательным контроллером. Авторы используют латентное представление и диффузионные модели для моделирования неопределённой динамики, что позволяет быстро генерировать вероятностные прогнозы траекторий. Ключевое преимущество заключается в возможности использовать модель в реальном времени благодаря использованию одного шага диффузии как при обучении, так и при выводе. Экспериментальные результаты показывают компромисс между способностью модели захватывать мультимодальность в динамике и качеством управления в среде CarRacing.

en.title: Fast and Uncertain Dynamics Prediction for Reinforcement Learning
en.desc: Value Diffusion World Models (Valdi) introduce a novel approach to enhance Model Predictive Control (MPC) in reinforcement learning by integrating latent diffusion dynamics. This method allows for rapid and uncertain dynamics prediction, which is crucial for real-time decision-making. By utilizing a single diffusion step during both training and inference, Valdi achieves performance comparable to traditional deterministic models while maintaining the ability to model uncertainty. The research highlights a balance between the richness of predictive multimodality and the effectiveness of control performance in dynamic environments.

zh.title: 价值扩散世界模型：快速预测不确定动态的创新
zh.desc: 本文提出了价值扩散世界模型（Valdi），结合了端到端的在线训练和潜在扩散动态，以实现快速且不确定的动态预测，适用于强化学习中的模型预测控制（MPC）。扩散模型能够有效建模不确定的动态，但其迭代推理过程使得低延迟的潜在规划变得困难。通过在CarRacing环境中的初步实验，Valdi在训练和推理中仅使用一步扩散，达到了确定性多层感知机基线的性能。实验结果揭示了预测多模态性与控制性能之间的权衡。

papers.title: Autonomous Scientific Discovery via Iterative Meta-Reflection
papers.abstract: Abstract An autonomous scientific discovery framework uses large language models and dynamic code generation to conduct open-ended research while maintaining statistical rigor through meta-reflection and multimodal data processing.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Autonomous scientific discovery systems offer the potential to accelerate research by automating the process of hypothesis generation and validation. However, current systems operate within constrained search spaces or require predefined research questions, limiting their capacity for true open-ended inquiry. Furthermore, while they generate hypotheses iteratively, they largely lack the ability to explicitly synthesize their own accumulated findings to uncover complex, interconnected phenomena. We introduce DiscoPER, an autonomous large language model-powered framework that conducts open-ended research by dynamically generating and executing code to explore datasets without pre-specified research objectives. To ensure rigorous scientific validity, every proposed discovery must pass statistical testing. To overcome the limitations of isolated search, our framework introduces a second-order reasoning mechanism that periodically analyzes its own accumulated discoveries. By treating prior discoveries as empirical data, DiscoPER identifies structural patterns, confounds, and epistemic gaps, actively redirecting hypothesis exploration toward uncharted regions of the search space. The search space is further expanded by incorporating tool use, enabling the system to explore hypotheses beyond structured metadata by seamlessly processing and extracting useful information from multimodal sources like images. Evaluated on iNatDisco, a new multimodal ecological knowledge benchmark with pattern-level ground truth obtained from peer-reviewed literature, DiscoPER recovers 8 of 9 known patterns with a 72.7% hypothesis support rate, outperforming both classical causal discovery and LLM-guided baselines. Ablations show that DiscoPER scales with more data, and confirms the benefits of second-order meta-reflection.

ru.title: Самоанализирующийся ИИ для открытых научных открытий
ru.desc: В работе представляется DiscoPER — фреймворк на основе больших языковых моделей для автономного научного открытия, который динамически генерирует и выполняет код для исследования данных без предзаданных гипотез. Система обеспечивает научную строгость, требуя статистической валидации каждого предложенного открытия через тестирование гипотез. Ключевой инновацией является механизм мета-рефлексии второго порядка, который периодически анализирует накопленные результаты для выявления структурных паттернов, скрытых переменных и пробелов в знаниях, переориентируя поиск в новые области. Фреймворк расширяет область поиска через использование инструментов и мультимодальной обработки данных, включая анализ изображений, и демонстрирует превосходство над классическими методами каузального вывода.

en.title: Unlocking Open-Ended Research with DiscoPER
en.desc: This paper presents DiscoPER, an innovative framework for autonomous scientific discovery that leverages large language models and dynamic code generation. Unlike traditional systems, DiscoPER conducts open-ended research without predefined questions, allowing for more exploratory hypothesis generation. It incorporates a second-order reasoning mechanism to analyze its own findings, identifying patterns and gaps in knowledge to guide further exploration. The framework has been tested on a new ecological benchmark, demonstrating superior performance in recovering known patterns compared to existing methods.

zh.title: 自主科学发现的新纪元
zh.desc: 这篇论文介绍了一种名为DiscoPER的自主科学发现框架，利用大型语言模型和动态代码生成进行开放式研究。该系统能够自动生成和验证假设，克服了传统方法在研究问题预设上的局限性。DiscoPER通过二阶推理机制分析自身的发现，识别结构模式和知识空白，从而引导探索新的假设。通过处理多模态数据，该框架在生态知识基准测试中表现优异，显示出其在科学研究中的潜力。

papers.title: Seed2.0 Model Card: Towards Intelligence Frontier for Real-World Complexity
papers.abstract: Abstract Seed2.0 addresses complex real-world tasks by tackling long-tail knowledge and complex instruction following challenges while enhancing reasoning, visual understanding, and search capabilities through a robust evaluation framework grounded in user needs.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct We present Seed2.0, a model series that takes a meaningful step toward solving complex, real-world tasks. Our approach begins with identifying users' genuine needs and constructing a reliable, forward-looking evaluation system by selecting and abstracting benchmarks grounded in these needs and in realistic, complex scenarios. Guided by this evaluation system, Seed2.0 targets two persistent challenges, long-tail knowledge and complex instruction following, substantially improving the model's reliability on intricate, long-horizon tasks. Beyond these, Seed2.0 delivers world-leading reasoning intelligence, visual understanding, and search capabilities that address the most common needs of a broad user base. Through extensive real-world use cases documented in this model card, we demonstrate that Seed2.0 begins to exhibit the ability to handle initial complex real-world tasks, delivering greater value to hundreds of millions of users.

ru.title: От теории к практике: модель для решения реальных сложных задач
ru.desc: В статье представлена модель Seed2.0, которая решает сложные задачи реального мира путём обработки редких знаний и следования сложным инструкциям. Авторы разработали надёжную систему оценки, основанную на реальных потребностях пользователей и сценариях использования. Модель демонстрирует передовые способности в логическом рассуждении, визуальном понимании и поиске информации. Seed2.0 показывает способность справляться с комплексными задачами на длинных горизонтах взаимодействия, обеспечивая практическую ценность для миллионов пользователей.

en.title: Empowering Real-World Solutions with Seed2.0
en.desc: Seed2.0 is a series of machine learning models designed to tackle complex real-world tasks by focusing on long-tail knowledge and intricate instruction following. The model enhances reasoning, visual understanding, and search capabilities through a user-centered evaluation framework. By identifying genuine user needs, Seed2.0 constructs benchmarks that reflect realistic scenarios, improving the model's reliability on challenging tasks. This approach aims to provide significant value to a wide user base by demonstrating the model's effectiveness in handling complex tasks.

zh.title: Seed2.0：解决复杂任务的智能模型
zh.desc: Seed2.0 是一个针对复杂现实任务的模型系列，旨在解决长尾知识和复杂指令跟随的挑战。该模型通过构建一个基于用户需求的评估框架，提升推理能力、视觉理解和搜索能力。Seed2.0 通过识别用户的真实需求，选择和抽象基准，来构建一个可靠的评估系统。通过大量的现实案例，Seed2.0 展示了其处理复杂任务的能力，为数亿用户提供了更大的价值。

papers.title: When LLMs Read Tables Carelessly: Measuring and Reducing Data Referencing Errors
papers.abstract: Abstract Large language models exhibit data referencing errors when processing tables, which can be mitigated through critic-based filtering and rejection sampling, with a lightweight 4B-parameter model achieving high detection accuracy.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct While large language models (LLMs) perform well on table tasks, they still make data referencing errors (DREs), i.e., incorrectly citing or omitting table values, despite understanding the table structure. Beyond final-answer accuracy, DREs directly compromise the correctness and reliability of intermediate reasoning steps. Yet prior studies have only offered limited, small-scale analyses. In this work, we present the first systematic evaluation of tabular data referencing errors across different models and tasks. Our results show that DREs occur across all tested models (1.7B to 20B parameters). Furthermore, we demonstrate that incorporating data referencing as a critic significantly improves answer accuracy up to 12.0%, through critic-based filtering and rejection sampling. Finally, we trained a lightweight 4B-parameter critic model that achieves an average F1 score of 78.2% in detecting both in-distribution and out-of-distribution DREs, and effectively assists inference for larger models.

ru.title: Критик для коррекции ошибок при работе с табличными данными в больших языковых моделях
ru.desc: В работе исследуются ошибки при обращении к табличным данным в больших языковых моделях, которые могут неправильно цитировать или пропускать значения из таблиц несмотря на понимание структуры. Авторы показали, что такие ошибки присутствуют во всех протестированных моделях размером от 1.7B до 20B параметров. Они предложили использовать критик-модель для фильтрации и rejection sampling, что улучшает точность ответов на 12%. Легковесная 4B-параметровая критик-модель достигает F1-меры 78.2% при обнаружении ошибок и эффективно помогает при инференсе больших моделей.

en.title: Enhancing Table Accuracy in Language Models with Critic-Based Filtering
en.desc: This paper addresses the issue of data referencing errors (DREs) in large language models (LLMs) when they process tabular data. DREs occur when these models incorrectly cite or omit values from tables, which affects the reliability of their reasoning. The authors conducted a systematic evaluation of DREs across various models and tasks, revealing that these errors are prevalent even in models with up to 20 billion parameters. They propose a solution using a lightweight 4B-parameter critic model that enhances answer accuracy by up to 12% through critic-based filtering and rejection sampling, achieving a high detection accuracy for DREs.

zh.title: 提升大型语言模型的表格数据引用准确性
zh.desc: 大型语言模型在处理表格时会出现数据引用错误（DRE），即错误引用或遗漏表格值。尽管这些模型理解表格结构，但它们的中间推理步骤的正确性和可靠性受到影响。本文首次系统评估了不同模型和任务中的数据引用错误，结果显示所有测试模型均存在此问题。通过引入数据引用作为评判标准，结合过滤和拒绝采样的方法，我们的轻量级4B参数模型在检测DRE方面取得了78.2%的F1分数，显著提高了答案的准确性。

papers.title: AtomiMed: Hierarchical Atomic Fact-Checking for Universal Clinical-Aware Medical Report Evaluation
papers.abstract: Abstract AtomiMed presents a novel evaluation framework for medical report generation that decomposes clinical narratives into atomic facts and uses an agentic cross-verification process to improve accuracy assessment beyond traditional metrics.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Traditional metrics for Medical Report Generation (MRG) predominantly rely on surface-level n-gram overlap, which fails to capture clinical factual accuracy and often overlooks catastrophic diagnostic errors. We address this fundamental limitation by proposing AtomiMed, a universal, modality-agnostic evaluation framework that decomposes complex medical narratives into a standardized, multi-level hierarchy of Atomic Clinical Facts, encompassing Disease-level entities and Attribute-level descriptors, including location, morphology, and severity. By implementing an Agentic Cross-Verification loop between ground-truth and predicted reports, AtomiMed simulates a multi-radiologist peer-review process to verify clinical consistency, thus enabling the decoupled assessment of diagnostic detection and descriptive accuracy. To facilitate standardized evaluation, we introduce MRGEvalKit, an open-source toolkit for automated hierarchical extraction, and curate OmniMRG-Bench, a comprehensive multi-modal benchmark covering X-ray, CT, MRI, and Ultrasound. Extensive experiments on multiple expert-annotated reader studies demonstrate that AtomiMed achieves significantly higher correlation with human radiologist judgment compared to traditional and model-based metrics. Our code are release at https://github.com/Venn2336/MRGEvalkit

ru.title: Атомарная верификация: от поверхностной оценки к клинической точности в генерации медицинских отчётов
ru.desc: AtomiMed представляет новую систему оценки для генерации медицинских отчётов, которая разбивает клинические нарративы на атомарные факты. Авторы используют агентный процесс кросс-верификации между эталонными и сгенерированными отчётами для имитации рецензирования несколькими радиологами. Предложенный подход позволяет декодировать оценку точности диагностического обнаружения и описательной точности на многоуровневой иерархии клинических фактов. Эксперименты показывают, что AtomiMed достигает значительно более высокой корреляции с суждениями человеческих радиологов по сравнению с традиционными метриками на основе n-граммов.

en.title: Revolutionizing Medical Report Evaluation with Atomic Facts
en.desc: AtomiMed introduces a new framework for evaluating medical report generation by breaking down clinical narratives into smaller, precise facts called Atomic Clinical Facts. This approach goes beyond traditional evaluation methods that only measure surface similarities, which can miss critical diagnostic errors. By using an Agentic Cross-Verification process, AtomiMed mimics a peer-review system among radiologists to ensure the accuracy of both diagnosis and descriptions in reports. The framework is supported by an open-source toolkit and a benchmark dataset, showing improved alignment with expert radiologist assessments compared to existing metrics.

zh.title: AtomiMed：提升医疗报告生成准确性的创新评估框架
zh.desc: AtomiMed提出了一种新的医疗报告生成评估框架，旨在将临床叙述分解为基本事实，并通过代理交叉验证过程提高准确性评估。传统的医疗报告生成评估主要依赖表面级的n-gram重叠，无法有效捕捉临床事实的准确性，常常忽视严重的诊断错误。AtomiMed通过将复杂的医疗叙述分解为标准化的多层次原子临床事实，解决了这一根本性限制。我们还推出了MRGEvalKit工具包和OmniMRG-Bench基准，以支持自动化的层次提取和多模态评估。
